amber.description: Catalysts, regulatory proteins, molecular machines, receptors

black.description: Terminal products, cellular outcomes, equilibrium states, system endpoints

darkSkyBlue.description: Biochemical reactions, transport, modifications, transcription, translation

green.description: External signals, nutrient availability, environmental conditions, initial states

lightCyan.description: Metabolites, molecular complexes, cellular states, compounds

purple.description: Multi-component requirements, signal integration, convergence points

yellow.description: Decision points with alternative pathways (yes/no branching)

description: The heat shock response is a universal cellular defense against protein-damaging stress. In E. coli, this response is mediated by the alternative sigma factor σ32 (RpoH), which directs RNA polymerase to heat shock gene promoters. The system includes sophisticated regulation at transcriptional, translational, and protein stability levels, with feedback control through DnaK-DnaJ-GrpE chaperone system titration by misfolded proteins.

name: Heat Shock Response and Protein Quality Control
notes: This flowchart represents the heat shock response with 38 unique nodes (A-BL). Contains 2 OR logic gates (temperature threshold, feedback assessment) and 2 AND gates (DnaK-DnaJ system requires both components, GroEL-GroES system requires both). The system demonstrates multilevel regulation (transcriptional, translational, protein stability), positive feedback (misfolded proteins induce response), and negative feedback (restored proteins shut down response). Two major chaperone systems work in parallel. All mechanisms validated against structural and biochemical studies.
scientificAccuracy: This flowchart is based on verified research on the heat shock response spanning four decades. The σ32-mediated regulatory mechanism and chaperone systems (DnaK/DnaJ, GroEL/GroES) are among the most thoroughly studied stress response pathways. All regulatory mechanisms have been validated through genetic, biochemical, and structural studies.
